rationale: Full lens coverage — blind, edge-case, and acceptance all ran successfully. Acceptance: all 6 acceptance criteria pass (diagnostics-only; no auth-behavior change; no secrets in any new log/beacon field). Edge-case: zero defects; verified beacon correctness (fires in all branches incl. the silent error branch, never throws, fires before window.close), same-origin under connect-src 'self', _safe_target_origin never raises, CSP scope limited to the mcp_auth callback pages, and frontend control flow unchanged. Blind flagged CWE-117 log injection on the new unauthenticated POST /oauth2/callback-diagnostics endpoint. Triage: the risk is mitigated in PRODUCTION by the logger's process_record_msg (json.dumps(msg)[1:-1] escapes CR/LF/quotes), but NOT in local env (config.is_local returns the message unescaped), and depending on a distant logging layer for a security property of a brand-new unauthenticated endpoint is fragile. One major security finding (CR-001, patch); 0 deferred, 0 dismissed. Recommend a 2-line source-level sanitization of the client-controlled string fields.

business_review.notes: sendDiagnostics fires in all 4 branches before window.close; endpoint logs all fields in one line.

business_review.criterion: AC2 — success-page-served log includes exact target_origin
business_review.notes: _build_success_callback_response logs target_origin from the same _derive_callback_target_origin used for the page's data-target-origin.

business_review.criterion: AC3 — listener logs windowOrigin+apiOrigin at setup and any mcp_auth_callback message (matched or dropped)
business_review.notes: listener-ready log + observed-message log gated on type before shape/origin checks.

business_review.criterion: AC4 — beacon not blocked by CSP for success and error
business_review.notes: connect-src 'self' added; sendBeacon fires on both pages (shared headers).

business_review.criterion: AC5 — no secret values in any new log/beacon
business_review.notes: presence booleans for code/state/error; origin-only via safeOrigin/_derive; extra='ignore'; 204 no body.

business_review.criterion: AC6 — no behavioral change to the auth flow
business_review.notes: postMessage + window.close preserved; backend exchange/store untouched; try/catch only guards the never-hit throw path.

standards_review.notes: All 6 commits across both repos use 'EPMCDME-13237: Capital description'.
Small focused functions, clear names, ruff/eslint clean on changed files, no duplication beyond the deliberate per-branch postMessage try/catch.

New unauthenticated endpoint logs client-controlled strings without source-level CR/LF/control-char neutralization (CWE-117). Mitigated in prod by the logger's json escaping, not in local env. See CR-001.

findings.problem: The unauthenticated POST /v1/mcp-auth/oauth2/callback-diagnostics endpoint interpolates client-controlled string fields (post_message_error ≤512, target_origin/auth_config_id ≤256, bridge_error_code/idp_error_code ≤128) directly into the INFO/WARNING log message with no CR/LF or control-character neutralization at the source. Pydantic max_length caps size but does not strip newlines.
findings.impact: An anonymous caller could embed newline-separated text in these fields to forge log lines (CWE-117). In production this is incidentally neutralized by the logger's process_record_msg (json.dumps escaping), but the local-env path returns the message unescaped, and a new unauthenticated endpoint should not rely on a distant logging layer for this security property.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize the client-controlled fields at the source before formatting the log line — e.g. replace CR/LF/control chars (or json.dumps each value) in a small helper applied to auth_config_id, target_origin, post_message_error, bridge_error_code, idp_error_code. Keeps the endpoint self-contained-safe regardless of logger config or environment.
